codeReview.summary: The workflow dialog refactor mostly aligns the forward path with the standard submit flow, but the new return dialog is not actually honored by the reviewer workbench. ReviewPanel still hard-codes every return to the designer node (`sj`), so jd/sh/pz users cannot execute the dialog's selected target-node path and the shipped UI/behavior contract diverges.

issues.description: `handleReturnToNode()` always calls `userStore.returnTaskToNode(currentTask.value.id, 'sj', ...)`, and the dialog confirm handler at line 829 discards the emitted `_targetNode`. That means the new standard return dialog never returns to the user-selected previous node, despite `WorkflowReturnDialog` advertising selectable prior nodes and the feature description promising standard dialog semantics without hidden legacy behavior. From `sh` or `pz`, the UI suggests the reviewer can step back one stage, but the code always jumps all the way to design.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `review-entry-worker` skill requires live reviewer browser smoke validation in step 9, but this worker still returned success after skipping it because port `3101` might belong to another checkout. The skill should either define an explicit fallback/return-to-orchestrator rule for this mission constraint or the mission docs should say automated-only completion is acceptable in that case.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/review-entry-worker/SKILL.md step 9 requires opening `http://127.0.0.1:3101/` and validating a task/action path, while the handoff for session `87cd126c-0ca3-403d-9f9a-0cc05c58a550` records `followedProcedure: false` and says browser smoke was skipped due to unrelated worktree changes / possible shared 3101 ownership. `AGENTS.md` also lists this as a return-to-orchestrator condition.

summary: Reviewed commit `eaa9f337e5da499b9acc0104c198bec783738e17`, the worker handoff, transcript skeleton, and shared mission docs/skill guidance. Result: fail, because the new reviewer return dialog does not actually control the return target in `ReviewPanel`, so the shipped behavior still relies on an implicit always-return-to-design path.
